nodes.name: run-migrations.js
nodes.summary: Standalone CLI script that applies pending SQL migration files to the PostgreSQL database, tracking which migrations have already been run in a migrations table.
nodes.languageNotes: Run directly via `node` rather than required as a module; has no module.exports and calls main().catch() at the bottom to handle and report failures before closing the pool.

nodes.name: streaming-excel.js
nodes.summary: Utility for generating large Excel reports (daily and date-range production reports) using ExcelJS's streaming writer so workbooks are written directly to the HTTP response without buffering the whole file in memory.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ExcelJS's WorkbookWriter streaming API to keep memory usage low for large daily/range exports, committing each sheet/row incrementally instead of building an in-memory workbook.

nodes.name: ensureMigrationsTable
nodes.summary: Creates the migrations tracking table if it does not already exist.

nodes.name: getAppliedMigrations
nodes.summary: Queries the migrations table to determine which migration files have already been applied.

nodes.name: getMigrationFiles
nodes.summary: Reads the migrations directory and returns the list of SQL migration files in order.

nodes.name: applyMigration
nodes.summary: Executes a single migration file's SQL against the database and records it as applied.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that orchestrates ensuring the migrations table exists, finding pending migration files, and applying them in order.

nodes.name: createStreamingWorkbook
nodes.summary: Creates an ExcelJS streaming WorkbookWriter piped directly to the HTTP response with the appropriate download headers.

nodes.name: addStreamingSheet
nodes.summary: Adds a worksheet to a streaming workbook and writes rows incrementally from a database query result, applying header styling.

nodes.name: exportDailyReportStreaming
nodes.summary: Streams a complete daily production report workbook (multiple sheets) directly to the HTTP response for a given date.

nodes.name: exportRangeReportStreaming
nodes.summary: Streams a production report workbook aggregated over a date range directly to the HTTP response.

nodes.name: db.config.js
nodes.summary: Configures and exports the shared PostgreSQL connection pool (pg.Pool) used across the entire backend, including a custom type parser that returns DATE columns as plain strings to avoid IST timezone off-by-one errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Overrides pg's default DATE (OID 1082) type parser globally so all queries in the app receive raw YYYY-MM-DD strings instead of UTC-shifted Date objects.

nodes.name: audit.js
nodes.summary: Express middleware and helper functions for recording and querying audit log entries (who changed what, when, from where) across the application's data tables.

nodes.name: transaction.js
nodes.summary: Database transaction helpers providing wrapped transaction execution, isolation level control, retry-on-conflict logic, savepoints, and row-level locking for PostgreSQL operations.

nodes.name: validation.js
nodes.summary: Zod-based request validation middleware defining schemas for all major entities (users, lines, employees, products, daily plans, progress, assignments) plus body/query/param validators and input sanitization.
nodes.languageNotes: Centralizes Zod schemas as a single exported `schemas` map plus reusable primitive validators (positiveInt, dateString, empCode, etc.) shared across route handlers.

nodes.name: getClientIP
nodes.summary: Extracts the originating client IP address from an Express request, accounting for proxy headers.

nodes.name: getUserAgent
nodes.summary: Reads the User-Agent header from an Express request for audit logging context.

nodes.name: getSessionId
nodes.summary: Resolves a session identifier from the request for correlating audit entries with a user session.

nodes.name: logAudit
nodes.summary: Writes a structured audit log entry (table, action, record id, actor, IP, timestamp) to the audit_logs table.

nodes.name: auditMiddleware
nodes.summary: Express middleware factory that wraps a route handler to automatically log create/update/delete actions on a given table.

nodes.name: getAuditHistory
nodes.summary: Retrieves the audit trail of changes for a specific table and record id.

nodes.name: getAuditSummary
nodes.summary: Produces an aggregated summary of recent audit activity for dashboards or admin review.

nodes.name: getRecentCriticalChanges
nodes.summary: Fetches the most recent high-impact audit log entries (e.g., deletions, critical field changes) for monitoring.

nodes.name: searchAuditLogs
nodes.summary: Performs a filterable, paginated search across the audit_logs table by table name, action, actor, or date range.

nodes.name: withTransaction
nodes.summary: Runs a callback within a BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK database transaction, releasing the client afterward.

nodes.name: withClient
nodes.summary: Acquires a pooled database client, runs a callback with it, and guarantees release back to the pool.

nodes.name: withIsolation
nodes.summary: Executes a transaction with an explicit SQL isolation level (e.g., SERIALIZABLE) for stronger consistency guarantees.

nodes.name: withRetry
nodes.summary: Retries a transactional operation on serialization/deadlock failures with backoff, used for concurrency-sensitive writes.

nodes.name: createSavepoint
nodes.summary: Issues a SQL SAVEPOINT within an active transaction for partial rollback support.

nodes.name: rollbackToSavepoint
nodes.summary: Rolls back a transaction to a previously created savepoint.

nodes.name: releaseSavepoint
nodes.summary: Releases a savepoint after the partial operation it guarded has succeeded.

nodes.name: transactionMiddleware
nodes.summary: Express middleware that attaches transaction helpers to the request/response cycle for downstream route handlers.

nodes.name: lockForUpdate
nodes.summary: Issues a SELECT ... FOR UPDATE to lock a single row by column value for the duration of a transaction.

nodes.name: lockRowsForUpdate
nodes.summary: Issues a SELECT ... FOR UPDATE to lock multiple rows matching a set of column values within a transaction.

nodes.name: validateBody
nodes.summary: Express middleware factory that validates req.body against a given Zod schema and returns a 400 with details on failure.

nodes.name: validateQuery
nodes.summary: Express middleware factory that validates req.query against a given Zod schema and returns a 400 with details on failure.

nodes.name: validateParams
nodes.summary: Express middleware factory that validates req.params against a given Zod schema and returns a 400 with details on failure.

nodes.name: sanitizeInputs
nodes.summary: Global Express middleware that recursively sanitizes string fields in incoming request bodies to mitigate injection/XSS risks.

nodes.name: api.routes.js
nodes.summary: The single, monolithic Express router (~16,200 lines, 152 routes) implementing nearly all backend API endpoints for the WorkSync MES: production line planning, workstation assignment, hourly progress tracking, overtime, changeover, audit, line balancing, Excel import/export, and WiFi configuration.
nodes.languageNotes: Extremely large single-file router (152 router.get/post/put/patch/delete handlers) mixing pure helper functions (date/time normalization, workstation history reconciliation, changeover snapshot logic) with inline route handlers; relies on db.config's pool, transaction/validation/audit middleware, realtime SSE broadcast, and qr utilities.

nodes.name: normalizePlanMonth
nodes.summary: Normalizes various plan-month input formats (ISO, slash-separated, Date) into a canonical YYYY-MM string.

nodes.name: normalizeWorkDate
nodes.summary: Normalizes a work date from multiple accepted formats (ISO, slash, dash, Date object) into canonical YYYY-MM-DD, used throughout the planning and progress endpoints.

nodes.name: collectDuplicateEmployeeIds
nodes.summary: Scans a list of assignment items to find employee IDs that appear more than once, used to reject conflicting workstation assignments.

nodes.name: resolveHistoryEffectiveFromHour
nodes.summary: Computes the next working hour slot (or attendance start) from which an assignment history record becomes effective.

nodes.name: closeAssignmentHistoryForEmployee
nodes.summary: Closes the open assignment-history window for an employee (sets effective_to_hour) when they are unlinked from a workstation.

nodes.name: syncAssignmentHistoryForCurrentRow
nodes.summary: Creates or updates an employee_workstation_assignment_history row to reflect the current link state for a workstation assignment, supporting accurate per-hour output attribution.

nodes.name: closeHistoryForWorkstationAssignmentIfNeeded
nodes.summary: Closes an existing assignment-history window for a workstation when a different employee is taking over, preserving correct attribution before the handover.

nodes.name: clearEmployeeAssignmentConflicts
nodes.summary: Removes or unassigns conflicting existing workstation assignments for an employee on a given date before assigning them elsewhere.

nodes.name: findEmployeeAssignmentConflicts
nodes.summary: Finds existing workstation assignments for a set of employees on a date that would conflict with a new assignment.

nodes.name: findLatestPriorDailyPlanForLine
nodes.summary: Looks up the most recent daily plan for a line prior to a given date, used for carry-forward logic.

nodes.name: hasDailyPlanDeletionMarker
nodes.summary: Checks whether a line's daily plan for a date was explicitly marked deleted, to prevent unwanted carry-forward.

nodes.name: getLineActualOutput
nodes.summary: Computes the actual production output for a line on a given date by aggregating hourly progress and workstation records.

nodes.name: getProductTargetQuantity
nodes.summary: Fetches the configured target quantity for a product.

nodes.name: getCumulativeWorkstationOutput
nodes.summary: Aggregates cumulative output quantities per workstation for a line and date across all recorded hours.

nodes.name: getShiftWindowDetails
nodes.summary: Returns the configured shift start/end times and lunch break window from app_settings.

nodes.name: syncDailyPlanTargetFromWorkstations
nodes.summary: Recomputes and updates a line daily plan's target units based on the takt times of its planned workstations.

nodes.name: getEffectiveRegularSourceWorkstations
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective set of regular (non-OT) workstation plan rows that should be used for a line and date, accounting for changeover state.

nodes.name: getEffectiveRegularSourceProcesses
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective set of process rows tied to the regular workstation source for a line and date.

nodes.name: getEffectiveOtSourceWorkstations
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective set of overtime workstation rows for a line and date, merging OT plan overrides with the base plan.

nodes.name: recalculateOtPlanTarget
nodes.summary: Recomputes an overtime plan's target units based on its active OT workstations' takt times.

nodes.name: getWorkstationChangeoverSnapshot
nodes.summary: Captures a snapshot of workstation state relevant to an in-progress or pending product changeover.

nodes.name: buildChangeoverPromotionSnapshot
nodes.summary: Builds the full data snapshot needed to promote a changeover product to become the line's primary product.

nodes.name: normalizeRegularAssignmentsToProductWorkstations
nodes.summary: Reconciles employee_workstation_assignments rows so they align with the current product's workstation plan after a changeover.

nodes.name: ensureDailyPlanCarryForwardForLine
nodes.summary: Ensures a line has a daily plan for the current date by carrying forward the prior day's plan if none exists and no deletion marker is present.

nodes.name: ensureDailyPlansCarryForward
nodes.summary: Runs carry-forward plan creation across all production lines for a given work date.

nodes.name: getRegularAssignmentStateByEmployee
nodes.summary: Builds a lookup map of each employee's current regular workstation assignment state for a date, used to preserve link status across plan saves.

nodes.name: detachRegularAssignmentsFromPlan
nodes.summary: Detaches employee_workstation_assignments rows from a line_plan_workstations reference when a workstation plan is being replaced.

nodes.name: getPreservedRegularAssignmentState
nodes.summary: Looks up an employee's previously captured assignment state (link status, attendance) to restore it after a plan save.

nodes.name: formatHourRangeLabel
nodes.summary: Formats an hour slot integer into a human-readable hour-range label (e.g., '9-10 AM') for reports and UI.

nodes.name: getEmployeeProgressForWindow
nodes.summary: Computes per-employee production progress for an hour window, using assignment history to correctly attribute output even after mid-day workstation changes.

nodes.name: getDepartureHourDenomMap
nodes.summary: Builds a map of hour-slot denominators adjusted for worker departures, used to compute fair efficiency percentages.

nodes.name: applyDepartureEfficiency
nodes.summary: Applies departure-adjusted denominators to a set of progress rows to recompute accurate efficiency percentages.

nodes.name: getWorkstationProgressForWindow
nodes.summary: Computes per-workstation production progress for an hour window, aggregating output, targets, and efficiency.

nodes.name: getWorkstationOutputMap
nodes.summary: Builds a lookup map of recorded output quantities keyed by workstation id for a line and date.

nodes.name: getHourlyWorkstationEmployeeFallbackMap
nodes.summary: Builds a fallback map of which employee was at each workstation for a given hour when assignment history is incomplete.

nodes.name: logClientError
nodes.summary: Persists a client-side error report (payload plus request context) for later diagnostics.

nodes.name: syncProductProcessListFromSource
nodes.summary: Copies the product_processes list from a source product to a target product, used when duplicating or carrying forward a changeover product's process plan.

nodes.name: copyWorkstationPlanFromDate
nodes.summary: Copies an entire workstation plan (line_plan_workstations + processes) from one line/date/product to another, supporting plan carry-forward and duplication.

nodes.name: carryForwardMixedLineState
nodes.summary: Carries forward a line's full mixed state (regular + OT + changeover plans, assignments, and WIP) from a source date to a target date.

nodes.name: remapProcessMaterialWipBetweenWorkstations
nodes.summary: Remaps in-progress material/WIP quantities between workstations when a workstation plan changes which processes are grouped together.

nodes.name: ensureWorkstationPlanCarryForward
nodes.summary: Ensures a workstation plan exists for a line/date/product by carrying forward the latest prior plan if missing.

nodes.name: runLineBalancing
nodes.summary: Runs a takt-time greedy grouping algorithm over a list of processes to assign them into balanced workstations.

nodes.name: generateLinePlanTemplate
nodes.summary: Generates a full line workstation plan template (groups, workstations, processes) for a product, used by the line-balancing and plan-generation endpoints.

nodes.name: parseSupervisorQr
nodes.summary: Parses and validates a QR code payload scanned by a supervisor into its constituent fields (line, workstation, etc.).

nodes.name: getLineProductIds
nodes.summary: Resolves the set of product IDs currently associated with a line (primary plus any changeover product).

nodes.name: isProcessActiveForChangeover
nodes.summary: Determines whether a process should be considered active given the line's current changeover state.

nodes.name: resolveProcessForLine
nodes.summary: Resolves a process record for a line from a payload that may reference it by id, code, or sequence, validating it belongs to the line's active products.

nodes.name: resolveNextProcessForLine
nodes.summary: Finds the next sequential process after a given process for a line, used for WIP flow and feed calculations.

nodes.name: refreshProcessWip
nodes.summary: Recalculates and updates the work-in-progress quantity for a single process on a line and date.

nodes.name: refreshGroupWip
nodes.summary: Recalculates and updates work-in-progress quantities for all processes within a named group on a line and date.

nodes.name: getProductFeedSummary
nodes.summary: Summarizes cumulative feed quantity and remaining order quantity for a product across its order history.

nodes.name: getWorkstationFeedContext
nodes.summary: Gathers feed-related context (material provided, WIP, upstream output) for a specific workstation on a line and date.

nodes.name: validateWorkstationHourlySave
nodes.summary: Validates a supervisor's hourly output submission for a workstation, enforcing feed/material availability, shortfall-reason requirements, and lock state before allowing the save.

nodes.name: performChangeoverPromotion
nodes.summary: Executes the full promotion of a changeover product to become a line's primary product, updating plans, assignments, and progress records transactionally.

nodes.name: parseNmcliWifiLine
nodes.summary: Parses a single line of `nmcli` WiFi scan output into a structured network entry.

nodes.name: getWifiNetworks
nodes.summary: Invokes `nmcli` to scan and list available WiFi networks for the device's network-configuration endpoints.

nodes.name: getWifiProfilesBySsid
nodes.summary: Looks up saved `nmcli` WiFi connection profiles matching a given SSID, used to reconnect without re-entering credentials.

nodes.name: realtime.js
nodes.summary: Implements the server-sent events (SSE) hub for real-time UI updates: holds the connected-client set, broadcasts data_change events, and listens on a dedicated PostgreSQL connection for LISTEN/NOTIFY 'data_change' messages to auto-regenerate QR codes and push updates to clients.

nodes.name: system-update.routes.js
nodes.summary: Express router exposing admin endpoints to check git/deploy status, trigger a self-update (git pull + restart) job, and roll back to the latest backup tag/DB dump by shelling out to git and spawning background update scripts.

nodes.name: server.js
nodes.summary: Main application entry point: configures Express middleware (CORS, JSON, HTTPS redirect, static assets), implements custom cookie-based JWT-like auth (sign/verify token, role guards), mounts the API and system-update routers, starts HTTP/HTTPS listeners, wires systemd watchdog notify, and handles graceful shutdown of servers, DB pool, and realtime connections.

nodes.name: qr.js
nodes.summary: Utility module that generates labeled SVG QR codes for employees, production lines, and workstations, writing them to disk under the configured qrcodes directory and updating each entity's qr_code_path/qr_generated_at columns in the database.

nodes.name: handleEvents
nodes.summary: Express request handler that upgrades a connection to an SSE stream, registers the response object in the in-memory clients set, and deregisters it when the client disconnects.

nodes.name: broadcast
nodes.summary: Serializes an event name and payload to JSON and writes it as an SSE message to every currently connected client.

nodes.name: closeAllConnections
nodes.summary: Ends every open SSE response, clears the clients set, and closes the dedicated PostgreSQL LISTEN connection during graceful shutdown.

nodes.name: startDbListener
nodes.summary: Opens a dedicated pg Client, issues LISTEN data_change, and on each notification parses the payload, regenerates QR codes for newly inserted employees/lines/processes/operations, and broadcasts the change to SSE clients.
nodes.languageNotes: Calls qr.generateProcessQrById and qr.generateOperationQrById, which are not present among qr.js's exported functions — a likely latent bug where the QR auto-regeneration silently throws on INSERT of product_processes/operations.

nodes.name: getRepoValue
nodes.summary: Runs a git command synchronously in the project root and returns its trimmed stdout, swallowing errors as null.

nodes.name: resolveRollbackTarget
nodes.summary: Determines the git tag and database backup path to roll back to, validating that no update job is currently running and that a previous backup exists before allowing a rollback.

nodes.name: spawnJob
nodes.summary: Spawns a detached background Node script (update or rollback) by job id and unrefs the child process so it survives the parent request lifecycle.

nodes.name: verifyToken
nodes.summary: Validates a custom signed token's HMAC-SHA256 signature against AUTH_SECRET and checks its expiry, returning the decoded payload or null.

nodes.name: buildAuthCookie
nodes.summary: Builds the Set-Cookie header value for the auth token, applying HttpOnly/SameSite/Secure flags based on whether the request was made over HTTPS.

nodes.name: setupSystemdNotify
nodes.summary: Sends READY=1 to systemd via sd_notify on startup and schedules periodic WATCHDOG=1 pings at half the configured watchdog interval, enabling systemd service supervision.

nodes.name: gracefulShutdown
nodes.summary: Handles SIGTERM/SIGINT by closing the HTTP and HTTPS servers, ending the PostgreSQL pool, closing all realtime SSE connections, and force-exiting the process if shutdown does not complete within a timeout.

nodes.name: writeQrWithLabel
nodes.summary: Renders a QR code to SVG via the qrcode library, then post-processes the SVG to append one or two text labels beneath the code before writing the final file to disk.

nodes.name: generateEmployeeQrById
nodes.summary: Loads an employee by id, generates a labeled QR (employee code + name) under the employees QR directory, and updates the employee's qr_code_path and qr_generated_at columns.

nodes.name: generateLineQrById
nodes.summary: Loads a production line by id, generates a labeled QR (line code + name) under the lines QR directory, and updates the line's qr_code_path and qr_generated_at columns.

nodes.name: generateWorkstationQrForLine
nodes.summary: Generates a labeled QR code for every workstation belonging to a given production line, writing each SVG file and collecting the results to return to the caller.

nodes.name: run-system-rollback.js
nodes.summary: CLI script that rolls back a previously applied system update job: verifies a clean git worktree, restores the database from the most recent backup, resets the repository to the prior commit, and reports rollback status/log progress for a given job ID.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of Node's child_process.spawn with manual stdout/stderr stream buffering to drive git, pg_restore/psql, and gunzip subprocesses sequentially.

nodes.name: run-system-update.js
nodes.summary: CLI script that performs an in-place system update: checks out the latest commit, creates a rollback snapshot (db backup + commit ref), runs pre/post hook commands from the update plan, syncs environment files, and updates job status/log for a given job ID.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors run-system-rollback.js's command-running and git-status helpers, suggesting shared logic that could be further factored into utils.

nodes.name: system-update-plan.js
nodes.summary: Loads and normalizes the system update plan configuration (JSON or JS module), merges it with built-in defaults, syncs declared environment files into place, and executes the plan's pre/post hook shell commands.

nodes.name: system-update.js
nodes.summary: Low-level utility module providing the runtime state machinery for system update/rollback jobs: status JSON read/write, append-only log file, and a simple file-based lock to prevent concurrent update runs.

nodes.name: runCommand
nodes.summary: Spawns a child process for the given command/args, streaming and logging stdout/stderr line-by-line, and resolves with the collected output or rejects on non-zero exit.

nodes.name: ensureCleanWorktree
nodes.summary: Checks `git status --porcelain` output and throws if there are uncommitted changes outside the ignored runtime directories, preventing rollback on a dirty worktree.

nodes.name: formatStamp
nodes.summary: Formats the current date/time into a sortable `YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS` timestamp string used for backup/snapshot directory naming.

nodes.name: createSafetySnapshot
nodes.summary: Creates a pre-rollback safety snapshot directory recording the current git commit and the latest database backup path, so a failed rollback can itself be recovered from.

nodes.name: restoreDatabaseFromBackup
nodes.summary: Restores the PostgreSQL database from a gzip-compressed SQL backup by piping `gunzip` output into `psql`, streaming progress to the update log and resolving once both subprocesses complete successfully.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main orchestration function for the rollback script: acquires the job lock, verifies worktree cleanliness, restores the database, resets git to the prior commit, re-syncs environment files, runs hook commands, and finalizes job status.

nodes.name: runCommand
nodes.summary: Spawns a child process for the given command/args, streaming and logging stdout/stderr line-by-line, and resolves with the collected output or rejects on non-zero exit.

nodes.name: ensureCleanWorktree
nodes.summary: Checks `git status --porcelain` output and throws if there are uncommitted changes outside the ignored runtime directories, preventing an update on a dirty worktree.

nodes.name: formatStamp
nodes.summary: Formats the current date/time into a sortable `YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS` timestamp string used for backup/snapshot directory naming.

nodes.name: createRollbackPoint
nodes.summary: Creates a rollback-point snapshot before applying the update, capturing a fresh database backup and recording the pre-update commit so the update can later be reverted.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main orchestration function for the update script: acquires the job lock, validates worktree state, fetches/checks out the target commit, creates a rollback point, runs pre-update hooks, applies migrations/dependencies, syncs environment files, runs post-update hooks, and finalizes job status.

nodes.name: loadRawPlan
nodes.summary: Loads the raw system update plan from disk, supporting either a `.js` module export or a JSON file, returning null if no plan file exists.

nodes.name: loadSystemUpdatePlan
nodes.summary: Loads the raw plan (if any) and merges its environment-file and hook-command sections on top of the built-in default plan, returning the effective update plan.

nodes.name: syncEnvironmentFiles
nodes.summary: Copies each environment file declared in the update plan from its source path to its target path, creating target directories as needed and logging each sync action; returns the list of synced targets.

nodes.name: normalizeCommandEntry
nodes.summary: Normalizes a hook command entry from the plan (resolving relative paths in args and building a human-readable command label) into a consistent shape for execution.

nodes.name: runHookCommands
nodes.summary: Runs a sequence of pre/post hook commands defined in the update plan for a given stage (e.g. 'pre-update'), logging and executing each one via runCommand.

nodes.name: ensureRuntimeDirs
nodes.summary: Ensures the runtime directories used for update status, logs, and lock files exist on disk before they are read or written.

nodes.name: writeStatus
nodes.summary: Persists the current update/rollback job status object to the status JSON file.

nodes.name: readStatus
nodes.summary: Reads the current update/rollback job status object from the status JSON file.

nodes.name: appendLog
nodes.summary: Appends a timestamped message line to the update/rollback log file.

nodes.name: getLogTail
nodes.summary: Reads the update/rollback log file and returns the last N lines, used to surface recent progress to API consumers.

nodes.name: acquireLock
nodes.summary: Writes a lock file containing the supplied lock data, used to prevent concurrent update or rollback jobs from running simultaneously.

nodes.name: readLock
nodes.summary: Reads and returns the current lock file contents, or null if no update/rollback job is currently locked.

nodes.name: releaseLock
nodes.summary: Removes the lock file, releasing the update/rollback job lock so a new job can start.

nodes.name: line_process_hourly_progress
nodes.summary: Adds 16 performance-tuning indexes across hourly progress, attendance, assignment history, daily plans/metrics, material transactions, day locks, shift closures, process WIP, and audit logs tables to speed up common query patterns.

nodes.name: audit_logs
nodes.summary: Extends audit logging with IP/session indexes and adds two reporting views (audit summary, recent critical changes) for monitoring sensitive changes.

nodes.name: defect_types
nodes.summary: Introduces the defect and downtime tracking subsystem: defect_types/defect_log and downtime_reasons/downtime_log tables plus daily summary views and supporting indexes for quality and machine-downtime reporting.

nodes.name: defect_log
nodes.summary: Introduces the defect and downtime tracking subsystem: defect_types/defect_log and downtime_reasons/downtime_log tables plus daily summary views and supporting indexes for quality and machine-downtime reporting.

nodes.name: downtime_reasons
nodes.summary: Introduces the defect and downtime tracking subsystem: defect_types/defect_log and downtime_reasons/downtime_log tables plus daily summary views and supporting indexes for quality and machine-downtime reporting.

nodes.name: downtime_log
nodes.summary: Introduces the defect and downtime tracking subsystem: defect_types/defect_log and downtime_reasons/downtime_log tables plus daily summary views and supporting indexes for quality and machine-downtime reporting.

nodes.name: products
nodes.summary: Adds a target_qty column (with index) to the products table so each product can carry its own production target quantity.

nodes.name: line_process_hourly_progress
nodes.summary: Adds a shortfall_reason column to line_process_hourly_progress so supervisors can record why hourly output fell below target.

nodes.name: employee_workstation_assignments
nodes.summary: Creates the core employee_workstation_assignments table linking employees to workstations on a line, with supporting indexes — the foundation of the live workstation assignment model.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstations
nodes.summary: Creates line_plan_workstations and line_plan_workstation_processes tables to model the planned workstation layout and process sequencing per line/date/product, with supporting indexes.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstation_processes
nodes.summary: Creates line_plan_workstations and line_plan_workstation_processes tables to model the planned workstation layout and process sequencing per line/date/product, with supporting indexes.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstations
nodes.summary: Adds an index supporting group_name lookups on the workstation plan table.

nodes.name: line_workstations
nodes.summary: Creates the line_workstations table storing static workstation codes and QR code paths per production line.

nodes.name: line_daily_plans
nodes.summary: Adds overtime_minutes and overtime_target columns to line_daily_plans, enabling an optional overtime session alongside the regular shift for a given line/date.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstations
nodes.summary: Adds a uniqueness index on line_plan_workstations scoped by line, date, and product to prevent duplicate workstation plan rows for the same plan context.

nodes.name: employee_workstation_assignments
nodes.summary: Adds an is_overtime-aware unique index to employee_workstation_assignments so regular and OT assignments for the same workstation can coexist without conflict.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstations
nodes.summary: Adds an is_ot_skipped boolean to line_plan_workstations so IE can mark individual workstations as not running during overtime.

nodes.name: line_daily_plans
nodes.summary: Adds a changeover_started_at timestamp to line_daily_plans to track when a supervisor activates a product changeover on a line.

nodes.name: line_ot_plans
nodes.summary: Creates the overtime planning subsystem tables: line_ot_plans, line_ot_workstations, and line_ot_workstation_processes, mirroring the regular workstation plan structure for OT sessions.

nodes.name: line_ot_workstations
nodes.summary: Creates the overtime planning subsystem tables: line_ot_plans, line_ot_workstations, and line_ot_workstation_processes, mirroring the regular workstation plan structure for OT sessions.

nodes.name: line_ot_workstation_processes
nodes.summary: Creates the overtime planning subsystem tables: line_ot_plans, line_ot_workstations, and line_ot_workstation_processes, mirroring the regular workstation plan structure for OT sessions.

nodes.name: line_ot_progress
nodes.summary: Creates the line_ot_progress table for recording supervisor-entered overtime output per OT workstation per date.

nodes.name: production_lines
nodes.summary: Adds a line_leader column to production_lines to record the assigned line leader's name.

nodes.name: employee_workstation_assignments
nodes.summary: Adds an is_linked boolean to employee_workstation_assignments distinguishing a confirmed (linked) employee presence from a mere assignment.

nodes.name: product_processes
nodes.summary: Adds a uniqueness index enforcing one sequence_number per product in product_processes, fixing a constraint gap that previously allowed duplicate sequence numbers.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstations
nodes.summary: Adds ws_changeover_active and ws_changeover_started_at columns to line_plan_workstations enabling per-workstation changeover so individual stations can switch product independently of the rest of the line.

nodes.name: products
nodes.summary: Adds a plan_month column (YYYY-MM format) to products for monthly production planning grouping.

nodes.name: group_wip
nodes.summary: Creates the group_wip table tracking work-in-progress quantity per process group per line/date, with a supporting index.

nodes.name: employee_workstation_assignments
nodes.summary: Changes the is_linked default to false and resets today's links so that assignments never auto-link employees — linking now requires explicit supervisor confirmation each morning.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstation_processes
nodes.summary: Adds an osm_checked boolean to line_plan_workstation_processes so IE/Admin can flag specific processes as OSM (operation sequence/measurement) observation points for the OSM report.

nodes.name: employee_workstation_assignments
nodes.summary: Adds a material_provided integer column to employee_workstation_assignments to record units of material/WIP given to a workstation at the start of the day.

nodes.name: employee_workstation_assignments
nodes.summary: Makes material_provided nullable (removing the default) so NULL distinctly represents 'not yet confirmed by supervisor' versus an explicit 0, and backfills previously auto-defaulted rows to NULL.

nodes.name: worker_departures
nodes.summary: Creates the worker_departures table to record when and why an employee left a workstation mid-day (sick, personal, operational, other), with supporting indexes.

nodes.name: worker_adjustments
nodes.summary: Creates the worker_adjustments table recording how a vacated workstation was reassigned in response to a worker departure, linked back to worker_departures.

nodes.name: product_processes
nodes.summary: Moves OSM-checked state from the daily plan level to product_processes so OSM selections persist per product rather than per day, and backfills existing checked flags accordingly.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstations
nodes.summary: Adds co_employee_id to line_plan_workstations so IE can pre-assign a suggested changeover employee per workstation, independent of live employee_workstation_assignments.

nodes.name: line_ot_plans
nodes.summary: Adds a supervisor_authorized flag to line_ot_plans so IE can grant supervisors permission to manage workstations and employees during an OT session.

nodes.name: employee_workstation_assignments
nodes.summary: Adds linked_at, late_reason, and attendance_start columns plus a check constraint to employee_workstation_assignments to track when supervisors confirm links, capture late-arrival reasons, and compute the effective shift start used for efficiency calculations; backfills existing linked rows.

nodes.name: employee_workstation_assignment_history
nodes.summary: Creates the employee_workstation_assignment_history table that records every assignment window (effective_from_hour to effective_to_hour) per employee/workstation/line/date, enabling correct attribution of hourly output even after mid-day reassignment or unlinking.

nodes.name: app_settings
nodes.summary: Seeds default working-hour app_settings (08:00-17:00 shift with a 60-minute lunch break) used as the factory-wide default schedule.

nodes.name: workstation_changeover_events
nodes.summary: Creates the workstation_changeover_events table capturing per-workstation changeover activation details (incoming product/workstation, assigned employee, feed quantity, start time), with supporting indexes.

nodes.name: line_ot_workstations
nodes.summary: Adds source-state and WIP snapshot columns to line_ot_workstations and line_ot_progress, preserving shift-end context for OT and keeping OT WIP separate from regular-shift WIP while supporting combined-flow views.

nodes.name: line_daily_plan_delete_markers
nodes.summary: Creates the line_daily_plan_delete_markers table to record soft-deletion of daily plans (who deleted, when) with a supporting index.

nodes.name: changeover_primary_promotions
nodes.summary: Creates the changeover_primary_promotions table recording when a changeover product is promoted to become the new primary product on a line, including a snapshot of prior state.

nodes.name: material_feed_events
nodes.summary: Creates the material_feed_events table logging material/WIP feed quantities given to a workstation group, including override tracking when feed exceeds the order quantity.

nodes.name: line_ot_workstations
nodes.summary: Changes the is_active default on line_ot_workstations to false so IE must explicitly activate each OT workstation rather than having it active by default.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstations
nodes.summary: Adds an is_custom_takt boolean to line_plan_workstations so the frontend can distinguish manually-overridden workstation takt times from auto-calculated ones tied to the live line target.

nodes.name: admin.html
nodes.summary: SPA shell for the Admin Panel: renders a fixed sidebar (Dashboard, Hourly Update, Users, Production Days, Audit Logs, Master Data, Reports, System) plus modal/toast containers, and loads admin.js to dynamically populate #main-content based on role 'admin'.

nodes.name: ie.html
nodes.summary: SPA shell for the Industrial Engineering (IE) Panel: sidebar covering Daily Plan, Line Management, Styles/Products, Reports (OSM, Plan History, Efficiency, Worker Efficiency, Downtime) and a custom 'Low Output Alerts' floating action button/modal with inline CSS, loaded via admin.js and low-output-alerts.js under role 'ie'.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Login/role-selection landing page ('WorkSync Access') presenting Admin, IE, Supervisor, and Management role cards that open a password login modal; styled inline and driven by login.js.

nodes.name: management.html
nodes.summary: SPA shell for the Management view: sidebar with Dashboard and Reports (Material Tracking, OSM, Efficiency, Worker Efficiency, Graphs), loaded via management.js under role 'management' for overview/reporting access.

nodes.name: supervisor.html
nodes.summary: SPA shell for the Supervisor (Line Leader) Panel: sidebar with Mapping, Feed Input, Hourly Procedure, and Worker Adjustment sections, including jsQR library for QR scanning and supervisor.js to drive the workstation assignment/output workflow under role 'supervisor'.

nodes.name: admin.css
nodes.summary: Primary design-system stylesheet for the admin dashboard, defining the leather/amber-gold CSS custom-property theme (colors, spacing) plus styles for the sidebar nav, mobile header, cards, stat widgets, data tables with sticky columns, forms, dropdown panels, modals, badges, and a floating action button.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a large `:root` custom-property palette (leather/amber theme) consumed throughout the file and likely shared conceptually with other panel stylesheets; organized into ~50 commented sections covering layout, tables, forms, and overlays.

nodes.name: ie.css
nodes.summary: Mobile-first stylesheet for the Industrial Engineer (IE) panel, styling the IE container, line-balancing/workstation-plan views, and related layout sections specific to the IE role.

nodes.name: offline.css
nodes.summary: Styles for offline/connectivity UI affordances: a fixed offline status indicator, sync-pending badge, offline banner, queued-action indicator, header connection status, and toast notifications for sync events, with mobile adjustments.

nodes.name: shop-floor.css
nodes.summary: Touch-optimized UX stylesheet for shop-floor screens, defining extra-large touch targets for gloved-hand interaction, action button states (success/error/warning/loading with animations), and enlarged quantity-input controls.
nodes.languageNotes: Extends the base design tokens with shop-floor-specific `:root` variables (e.g. `--touch-target-xl/xxl/giant`) tuned for industrial touch-screen use.

nodes.name: supervisor.css
nodes.summary: Mobile-first stylesheet for the Supervisor panel, covering the supervisor header/controls and the Morning/Hourly/Adjustment/Feed/OT/Shift-Summary section layouts plus status badge color variants.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template environment configuration listing server, database, security secrets, file-path, shift, and rate-limit settings required to run the WorkSync backend.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-pattern file for the understand-anything analysis tool, excluding secrets (.env, *.pem, *.key) and optionally test files from the knowledge-graph scan.

nodes.name: ecosystem.config.js
nodes.summary: PM2 process-manager configuration running the WorkSync server in 4-instance cluster mode with auto-restart, memory limits, log rotation paths, and an optional remote deploy target.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines both runtime (cluster/instances/memory limits) and deploy (git-based remote deploy) sections in one PM2 ecosystem file.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node.js package manifest for the WorkSync backend declaring start/migration scripts and core dependencies (Express, pg, ExcelJS, JWT, bcrypt, Joi, Zod, node-cron).

nodes.name: .graphifyignore
nodes.summary: Single-line ignore rule excluding the graphify-out/ directory from the graphify code-graph tool's own analysis.

nodes.name: app.config.js
nodes.summary: Empty placeholder file in the config directory with no current content.

nodes.name: rbac.js
nodes.summary: Role-based access control middleware defining the permission matrix and role hierarchy (admin, ie, supervisor, management) plus Express middleware factories for enforcing permissions and minimum roles on routes.

nodes.name: hasPermission
nodes.summary: Checks whether a given role has a specific permission according to the static PERMISSIONS map.

nodes.name: isRoleHigherOrEqual
nodes.summary: Compares two roles against the ROLE_HIERARCHY ordering to determine seniority.

nodes.name: getRolePermissions
nodes.summary: Returns the full list of permissions granted to a given role.

nodes.name: rbacMiddleware
nodes.summary: Core Express middleware that resolves the current user role and attaches permission-checking helpers to the request.

nodes.name: requirePermission
nodes.summary: Middleware factory returning a handler that rejects requests lacking a specific permission.

nodes.name: requireRole
nodes.summary: Middleware factory restricting a route to an explicit list of allowed roles.

nodes.name: requireMinRole
nodes.summary: Middleware factory restricting a route to roles at or above a minimum hierarchy level.

nodes.name: session.js
nodes.summary: In-memory session management middleware handling session creation, validation, renewal, cleanup, security headers, and rate limiting for the WorkSync Express app.

nodes.name: generateSessionId
nodes.summary: Generates a cryptographically random session identifier.

nodes.name: createSession
nodes.summary: Creates a new session record for an authenticated user, evicting the oldest session if the per-user limit is exceeded.

nodes.name: findOldestSession
nodes.summary: Finds the least-recently-used session among a user's active sessions for eviction.

nodes.name: getSession
nodes.summary: Looks up a session by its identifier.

nodes.name: touchSession
nodes.summary: Updates a session's last-activity timestamp.

nodes.name: destroySession
nodes.summary: Removes a session by id and clears it from the user's active session list.

nodes.name: destroyUserSessions
nodes.summary: Destroys all sessions belonging to a given user, used on logout-all or password change.

nodes.name: isSessionValid
nodes.summary: Validates a session against expiry and idle-timeout rules.

nodes.name: needsRenewal
nodes.summary: Determines whether a session is close enough to expiry to need a sliding renewal.

nodes.name: renewSession
nodes.summary: Extends a session's expiry window.

nodes.name: getUserActiveSessions
nodes.summary: Returns all currently active sessions for a user.

nodes.name: cleanupExpiredSessions
nodes.summary: Sweeps and removes expired sessions; invoked on an interval timer.

nodes.name: sessionMiddleware
nodes.summary: Express middleware that authenticates each request via its session cookie and attaches the user/session to the request object.

nodes.name: securityHeaders
nodes.summary: Express middleware applying security-related HTTP response headers.

nodes.name: rateLimiter
nodes.summary: Middleware factory implementing a sliding-window request rate limiter.

nodes.name: WorkSync_Operational_Guide.docx
nodes.summary: Word document operational guide describing how to use the WorkSync system, served as a static downloadable file from the public directory.

nodes.name: admin.css.bak
nodes.summary: Backup copy of the admin panel stylesheet (1578 lines), retained as a `.bak` file rather than the active CSS used by the admin UI.

nodes.name: admin.js
nodes.summary: Largest frontend script (~11,880 lines) implementing the entire admin panel: dashboard, line/product/process management, daily and OT plan editing, workstation plan upload, hourly progress and efficiency reporting, and system update/rollback controls.
nodes.languageNotes: Plain browser script (no ES module import/export) bootstrapped via a single DOMContentLoaded listener; organizes ~323 top-level functions by feature area (dashboard, plans, OT, reports, system updates) rather than classes.

nodes.name: requireAuth
nodes.summary: Verifies an authenticated admin session on page load and redirects to login if absent.

nodes.name: setupNavigation
nodes.summary: Wires up sidebar navigation link clicks to section switching.

nodes.name: setupMobileSidebar
nodes.summary: Sets up the collapsible sidebar behavior for mobile viewports.

nodes.name: loadSection
nodes.summary: Loads and renders the data for whichever admin section the user navigated to.

nodes.name: loadDashboard
nodes.summary: Loads and renders the main admin dashboard summary cards and line overview.

nodes.name: viewLineDetails
nodes.summary: Opens the detailed view for a single production line, loading its current plan and status.

nodes.name: openLineDetailsPage
nodes.summary: Builds and displays the full line-details page including plan, OT, and progress sections.

nodes.name: loadProducts
nodes.summary: Fetches and renders the products list/table in the admin products section.

nodes.name: viewProductProcess
nodes.summary: Displays the process/workstation breakdown for a selected product.

nodes.name: openPlanUploadModal
nodes.summary: Opens the modal for uploading an Excel-based daily plan.

nodes.name: submitPlanUpload
nodes.summary: Submits the uploaded Excel plan file to the plan-upload-excel API and handles the response.

nodes.name: loadDailyPlanData
nodes.summary: Loads the daily plan data for a selected line and date.

nodes.name: _buildLdTbody
nodes.summary: Builds the table body markup for the line-details workstation/process table.

nodes.name: ldPreviewCopyPlan
nodes.summary: Renders a preview of copying a workstation plan to another date/line before confirming.

nodes.name: renderLineDetailsContent
nodes.summary: Renders the full content panel for the line-details page, combining plan, employee, and OT data.

nodes.name: saveLineDetails
nodes.summary: Persists edits made on the line-details page back to the server.

nodes.name: fillOtLineCard
nodes.summary: Populates an overtime summary card for a line with target/actual figures.

nodes.name: printDailyPlans
nodes.summary: Generates a printable view of the daily plans across lines.

nodes.name: renderOTPlanDetails
nodes.summary: Renders the detailed OT plan view for a line and date.

nodes.name: _buildSingleOsmDailyCard
nodes.summary: Builds a single OSM (output-shipment-measure) daily report card.

nodes.name: _buildEfficiencyTable
nodes.summary: Builds the efficiency report table aggregating worker/workstation output against targets.

nodes.name: _buildWorkerIndividualEffTable
nodes.summary: Builds the per-worker individual efficiency breakdown table.

nodes.name: renderSystemUpdateSection
nodes.summary: Renders the system update/rollback admin section showing current version and available actions.

nodes.name: ie.js
nodes.summary: Frontend script for the Industrial Engineer (IE) attendance panel: loads, renders, edits, and saves daily employee attendance records with realtime updates.

nodes.name: loadAttendance
nodes.summary: Fetches and renders the attendance list for the selected date.

nodes.name: renderRow
nodes.summary: Renders a single attendance table row for an employee.

nodes.name: formatStatus
nodes.summary: Formats an attendance status code into a display label.

nodes.name: filterAttendance
nodes.summary: Filters the rendered attendance rows by search/status criteria.

nodes.name: saveRow
nodes.summary: Saves a single edited attendance row to the server.

nodes.name: saveAll
nodes.summary: Saves all pending attendance edits in bulk.

nodes.name: saveAttendance
nodes.summary: Performs the API call to persist attendance changes.

nodes.name: setupRealtime
nodes.summary: Subscribes to SSE events to keep the attendance view live-updated.

nodes.name: showToast
nodes.summary: Displays a transient toast notification message.

nodes.name: login.js
nodes.summary: Small frontend script controlling the login modal open/close behavior and submitting login credentials to the authentication API.

nodes.name: openLogin
nodes.summary: Opens the login modal dialog.

nodes.name: closeLogin
nodes.summary: Closes the login modal dialog.

nodes.name: submitLogin
nodes.summary: Submits the entered username/password to the login API and handles success/failure.

nodes.name: low-output-alerts.js
nodes.summary: Self-contained IIFE behavior module powering the low-output-alerts floating action button and modal, fetching and rendering summary/detail tabs of underperforming workstations against static HTML markup in ie.html.
nodes.languageNotes: Wrapped in an immediately-invoked function expression (IIFE) to avoid polluting globals; binds to pre-existing static DOM elements rather than generating its own shell.

nodes.name: escHtml
nodes.summary: Escapes a string for safe HTML interpolation.

nodes.name: updateBadge
nodes.summary: Updates the alert-count badge on the floating action button.

nodes.name: openModal
nodes.summary: Opens the low-output-alerts modal and triggers data refresh.

nodes.name: closeModal
nodes.summary: Closes the low-output-alerts modal.

nodes.name: renderModal
nodes.summary: Renders the modal shell and active tab content.

nodes.name: renderSummaryTab
nodes.summary: Renders the summary tab listing aggregated low-output alerts.

nodes.name: renderDetailsTab
nodes.summary: Renders the detailed tab listing individual workstation low-output entries.

nodes.name: fetchAlerts
nodes.summary: Fetches current low-output alert data from the API.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Initializes the low-output-alerts module, wiring up the FAB and modal event handlers.

nodes.name: management.js
nodes.summary: Frontend script (~2,455 lines) for the management dashboard: line metrics, material tracking, OSM and efficiency reports, and worker efficiency graphs, refreshed via realtime SSE events.

nodes.name: requireAuth
nodes.summary: Verifies an authenticated management session and redirects to login if absent.

nodes.name: setupMobileSidebar
nodes.summary: Sets up the collapsible sidebar behavior for mobile viewports.

nodes.name: setupRealtime
nodes.summary: Subscribes to SSE data-change events to keep the management dashboard live-updated.

nodes.name: loadMgmtMaterialTracking
nodes.summary: Loads material/WIP tracking data for the management material-tracking section.

nodes.name: refreshMgmtMaterialTracking
nodes.summary: Refreshes the rendered material tracking cards without a full reload.

nodes.name: buildMgmtMaterialGroupCard
nodes.summary: Builds the markup for a single material-tracking group card.

nodes.name: loadManagementDashboard
nodes.summary: Loads and renders the top-level management dashboard summary.

nodes.name: loadLineOptions
nodes.summary: Loads the list of production lines into a select dropdown.

nodes.name: loadLineMetrics
nodes.summary: Loads efficiency/output metrics for a selected line.

nodes.name: loadMgmtOsmReport
nodes.summary: Loads the OSM (output-shipment-measure) report data.

nodes.name: _buildMgmtOsmTable
nodes.summary: Builds the OSM report table markup.

nodes.name: _buildMgmtOsmRangeTable
nodes.summary: Builds the OSM report table for a date-range view.

nodes.name: loadMgmtEfficiencyReport
nodes.summary: Loads efficiency report data for the management efficiency section.

nodes.name: _buildMgmtEfficiencyTable
nodes.summary: Builds the full efficiency report table aggregating per-line/worker output against targets.

nodes.name: buildMgmtWorkerEfficiencyGraphs
nodes.summary: Builds chart data/markup for worker efficiency trend graphs.

nodes.name: loadMgmtWorkerIndividualEff
nodes.summary: Loads individual worker efficiency data.

nodes.name: buildMgmtWorkerIndividualEffTable
nodes.summary: Builds the individual worker efficiency breakdown table.

nodes.name: loadMgmtWorkerEfficiencyGraphs
nodes.summary: Loads the data feeding the worker efficiency graphs.

nodes.name: downloadReport
nodes.summary: Triggers download of a generated report file.

nodes.name: downloadRangeReport
nodes.summary: Triggers download of a date-range report file.

nodes.name: offline-db.js
nodes.summary: IndexedDB wrapper singleton (OfflineDB) providing local storage for a pending sync queue and cached API responses to support offline operation in the shop-floor UI.
nodes.languageNotes: Implemented as a plain object literal singleton (`const OfflineDB = {...}`) rather than a class, exposing methods like init/addToSyncQueue directly as object properties.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Opens (and upgrades if needed) the IndexedDB database, creating the sync-queue and cache object stores.

nodes.name: addToSyncQueue
nodes.summary: Adds a pending offline action to the sync queue store for later replay when connectivity returns.

nodes.name: offline-sync.js
nodes.summary: OfflineSync singleton managing online/offline detection, queuing actions while offline via OfflineDB, and background syncing of queued actions when connectivity is restored.
nodes.languageNotes: Plain object-literal singleton; depends on OfflineDB for persistence and exposes on/off listener registration for status changes.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Sets up online/offline event listeners and starts periodic background sync.

nodes.name: handleOnline
nodes.summary: Handles the browser coming back online: updates status and triggers a sync attempt.

nodes.name: handleOffline
nodes.summary: Handles the browser going offline: updates status indicator and notifies listeners.

nodes.name: updateStatusIndicator
nodes.summary: Updates the visible online/offline status indicator in the UI.

nodes.name: notifyListeners
nodes.summary: Notifies registered listeners of an offline/online status change event.

nodes.name: getStatus
nodes.summary: Returns the current online/offline and syncing status.

nodes.name: WorkSync_Pitch_Deck.docx
nodes.summary: Word document pitch deck describing WorkSync for stakeholders, served as a static downloadable file from the public directory.

nodes.name: optimistic-ui.js
nodes.summary: OptimisticUI singleton implementing optimistic update patterns: immediately reflects UI changes, queues the real API call with retry/rollback, and integrates with ShopFloorUX for visual feedback.
nodes.languageNotes: Object-literal singleton holding pending operations in a Map with bounded history for rollback; depends on ShopFloorUX for UI feedback hooks.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Initializes the optimistic UI manager and resumes any pending operations on load.

nodes.name: execute
nodes.summary: Executes an optimistic operation: applies the UI update immediately, then performs the real API call with rollback on failure.

nodes.name: executeWithRetry
nodes.summary: Wraps an API call with timeout and retry/backoff logic.

nodes.name: queueForSync
nodes.summary: Queues a failed optimistic operation for later retry via the offline sync mechanism.

nodes.name: updateListItem
nodes.summary: Applies an optimistic update to a list item element in the DOM.

nodes.name: updateCounter
nodes.summary: Applies an optimistic update to a counter/badge element, animating the value change.

nodes.name: createDebouncedUpdate
nodes.summary: Creates a debounced wrapper around an update function to coalesce rapid optimistic updates.

nodes.name: shop-floor-ux.js
nodes.summary: ShopFloorUX singleton providing touch-friendly visual feedback for factory-floor use: ripple effects, haptic feedback, quantity steppers, toasts, scan success/error flashes, and optimistic-update visual markers.
nodes.languageNotes: Object-literal singleton with ~25 methods covering touch/haptic UX; two standalone top-level functions (initTableScrollFade, initBottomNavSync) are defined outside the singleton for table/nav scroll behavior.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Initializes all shop-floor UX enhancements (touch ripple, haptics, steppers, HTTPS banner, error logging).

nodes.name: setupTouchRipple
nodes.summary: Adds a Material-style ripple animation to touch/click targets.

nodes.name: confirm
nodes.summary: Shows a confirm-style inline interaction requiring a deliberate second tap before committing an action.

nodes.name: toast
nodes.summary: Displays a transient toast notification.

nodes.name: markOptimisticPending
nodes.summary: Marks a DOM element as having a pending optimistic update.

nodes.name: markOptimisticSuccess
nodes.summary: Marks a DOM element as having a confirmed optimistic update.

nodes.name: rollbackOptimistic
nodes.summary: Reverts a DOM element's optimistic update visual state and re-applies the original value.

nodes.name: initTableScrollFade
nodes.summary: Initializes scroll-fade visual indicators on horizontally scrollable tables.

nodes.name: initBottomNavSync
nodes.summary: Synchronizes the active state of the bottom navigation bar with scroll position/route.

nodes.name: sse-manager.js
nodes.summary: SSEManager singleton wrapping a single EventSource connection to /events with exponential-backoff reconnection, heartbeat tracking, visibility/online-triggered reconnects, and a pub/sub listener API for data-change events.
nodes.languageNotes: Object-literal singleton intended as the single shared EventSource for the whole frontend; other scripts (admin.js, supervisor.js, management.js, ie.js) are expected to call SSEManager.on() rather than opening their own EventSource.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Initializes the SSE manager: connects, and wires visibility/online listeners to trigger reconnects.

nodes.name: connect
nodes.summary: Opens the EventSource connection to /events and attaches message/error handlers.

nodes.name: scheduleReconnect
nodes.summary: Schedules a reconnect attempt using exponential backoff up to a maximum delay/attempt count.

nodes.name: handleEvent
nodes.summary: Dispatches an incoming SSE event to registered listeners by event type.

nodes.name: on
nodes.summary: Registers a listener callback for a given SSE event type.

nodes.name: off
nodes.summary: Unregisters a previously registered listener.

nodes.name: onConnectionChange
nodes.summary: Registers a callback for connection status changes.

nodes.name: notifyConnectionChange
nodes.summary: Notifies registered connection-status listeners of a change.

nodes.name: updateStatusIndicator
nodes.summary: Updates the visible connection status indicator in the UI.

nodes.name: reconnect
nodes.summary: Forces an immediate reconnect attempt.

nodes.name: close
nodes.summary: Closes the EventSource connection and clears timers.

nodes.name: getStatus
nodes.summary: Returns the current connection status and reconnect-attempt count.

nodes.name: supervisor.js
nodes.summary: Largest supervisor-facing frontend script (~4,129 lines) implementing the line leader panel: QR-camera scanning, morning employee linking, hourly output entry, workstation changeover flows, adjustments, OT management, and shift-summary reporting.
nodes.languageNotes: Plain browser script bootstrapped on DOMContentLoaded; uses the device camera (getUserMedia) for QR scanning of employee/workstation codes alongside button-based employee linking.

nodes.name: requireAuth
nodes.summary: Verifies an authenticated supervisor session and redirects to login if absent.

nodes.name: setupNavigation
nodes.summary: Wires up sidebar navigation for the supervisor panel sections.

nodes.name: startCamera
nodes.summary: Starts the device camera stream for QR code scanning.

nodes.name: scanLoop
nodes.summary: Continuously scans video frames for QR codes during an active scan session.

nodes.name: loadMorningProcedure
nodes.summary: Loads the morning procedure section: workstation assignments grouped for the current line/date.

nodes.name: renderMorningAssignments
nodes.summary: Renders the morning assignment cards per workstation, including link buttons and status badges.

nodes.name: startChangeEmployee
nodes.summary: Opens the change-employee flow for a workstation, replacing the currently linked employee.

nodes.name: loadHourlyProcedure
nodes.summary: Loads the hourly procedure section: per-workstation targets and progress for the current hour.

nodes.name: onHourlyLineChange
nodes.summary: Handles switching the selected line in the hourly section, reloading plan and progress data.

nodes.name: renderHourlySummary
nodes.summary: Renders the full hourly summary table of workstation output vs. targets with shortfall reasons.

nodes.name: renderFeedInput
nodes.summary: Renders the feed-input section for entering material/WIP provided to workstations.

nodes.name: openWorkstationHourlyEntry
nodes.summary: Opens the entry form for recording a workstation's hourly output.

nodes.name: saveWorkstationHourlyOutput
nodes.summary: Saves the entered hourly output quantity (and shortfall reason if below target) for a workstation.

nodes.name: openWsChangeoverModal
nodes.summary: Opens the modal to start a changeover on a specific workstation, with employee picker.

nodes.name: submitWsChangeoverStart
nodes.summary: Submits the changeover-start request for a workstation with the selected employee.

nodes.name: renderOtPlanSection
nodes.summary: Renders the overtime plan section showing OT workstations, targets, and progress.

nodes.name: renderAdjStatusTable
nodes.summary: Renders the adjustment status table tracking worker departures and reassignments.

nodes.name: sw-register.js
nodes.summary: Tiny script that unregisters any previously installed service worker, effectively disabling the PWA service-worker feature.

nodes.name: line_plan_template.xlsx
nodes.summary: Downloadable Excel template matching the documented plan-upload format (LINE CODE, PRODUCT, BUYER, STYLE NO, DESCRIPTION, TARGET header rows plus GROUP/WORK STATION/EMPLOYEE CODE/PROCESS DETAILS table) used by admins/IE to prepare daily plan uploads.

nodes.name: sw.js
nodes.summary: Service worker script that unregisters itself and clears all caches on activation, completing the rollback away from PWA offline-caching behavior.

nodes.name: WorkSync_Technical_Report.docx
nodes.summary: Internal technical reference document (v1.0, March 2026) covering WorkSync's system overview, technology stack, user roles/permissions, the full 31+ table database schema, API endpoints, screens/panels, business flows, formulas, JS state, migration history, SSE events, Excel upload template, QR code system, PWA features, and key constants.
nodes.languageNotes: Served as a static asset under src/public/ rather than processed at runtime; functions as project-wide reference documentation rather than application code.

nodes.name: schema.base.migrations
nodes.summary: Plain-text manifest listing the ordered filenames of all 27 SQL migrations (007 through 033) that have been applied to bring the database to its current baseline state, used as a changelog/checklist rather than executable SQL.

nodes.name: app_settings
nodes.summary: Key/value store for global runtime configuration such as default working hours and lunch break minutes.

nodes.name: audit_logs
nodes.summary: Central audit trail recording every tracked change with old/new values, the acting user, request metadata (IP, user agent, session, path, method), and reason.

nodes.name: defect_log
nodes.summary: Per-employee, per-process defect occurrences including rework tracking (rework employee, completion time) and quality status.

nodes.name: defect_types
nodes.summary: Master list of defect type codes with category, severity, and whether the defect is reworkable.

nodes.name: downtime_log
nodes.summary: Records downtime incidents per line with start/end times, duration, affected processes, and resolution metadata.

nodes.name: downtime_reasons
nodes.summary: Master list of downtime reason codes, categorized and flagged as planned/unplanned with a default duration.

nodes.name: employee_attendance
nodes.summary: Daily attendance record per employee with in/out times and status.

nodes.name: employee_process_assignments
nodes.summary: Legacy table assigning employees directly to product processes per line; superseded by the workstation-based employee_workstation_assignments model but still present in the schema.

nodes.name: employee_workstation_assignments
nodes.summary: Core live-assignment table linking employees to workstations per line/date, tracking overtime flag, link confirmation state, late reason, attendance start, and material provided; unique per (line, date, workstation, overtime flag).

nodes.name: employees
nodes.summary: Employee master data including employee code, name, designation, default line, QR code path, active flag, and manpower factor.

nodes.name: group_wip
nodes.summary: Tracks work-in-progress quantities per process group per line/date, including materials-in and output quantity, used for group-first feed workstations.

nodes.name: line_daily_metrics
nodes.summary: Daily rollup of forwarded quantity, remaining WIP, materials issued, and QA output per line.

nodes.name: line_daily_plans
nodes.summary: One row per line per work date holding the active product, target units, lock state, changeover sequence/target, and overtime settings — the central daily planning record.

nodes.name: line_hourly_reports
nodes.summary: Free-text remarks captured per line per work date per hour slot.

nodes.name: line_material_stock
nodes.summary: Tracks opening/closing material stock and issued/used/returned totals per line per day.

nodes.name: line_ot_plans
nodes.summary: Overtime plan per line/date/product holding global OT minutes, OT target units, and a supervisor_authorized flag that grants the supervisor permission to manage OT workstations.

nodes.name: line_ot_progress
nodes.summary: Supervisor-entered OT output per workstation per date, including quantity, QA rejection, and the employee credited.

nodes.name: line_ot_workstation_processes
nodes.summary: Junction table linking OT workstations to product processes with sequence ordering.

nodes.name: line_ot_workstations
nodes.summary: Per-workstation OT configuration (active flag, OT minutes, actual SAM seconds) for a given OT plan.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstation_processes
nodes.summary: Junction table linking planned workstations to product processes with sequence ordering and an OSM-checked flag.

nodes.name: line_plan_workstations
nodes.summary: Workstation layout per line/date/product including takt time, actual SAM seconds, workload percentage, group name, OT-skip flag, changeover active state/start time, and the pre-assigned changeover employee (co_employee_id).

nodes.name: line_process_hourly_progress
nodes.summary: Hourly output entry per process per employee per line/date, including forwarded/remaining quantity, QA rejection, remarks, and mandatory shortfall reason when below target.

nodes.name: line_shift_closures
nodes.summary: Records when a supervisor closes a line's shift for a given work date, with closer identity and notes.

nodes.name: line_workstations
nodes.summary: Static workstation codes (e.g., W01-W100) defined per line, each with a QR code path.

nodes.name: material_transactions
nodes.summary: Logs material movement transactions per line/date between processes, including quantity, type, and recorder.

nodes.name: operations
nodes.summary: Reusable operation library (codes like OP-XXXX) shared across products, with category, active flag, and QR code path.

nodes.name: process_assignment_history
nodes.summary: Historical record of employee-to-process assignments per line with start/end times, quantity completed, and materials snapshot at link time — predecessor of the workstation-level assignment history table.

nodes.name: process_material_wip
nodes.summary: Tracks materials in/out and WIP quantity per process per line per date.

nodes.name: product_processes
nodes.summary: Ordered sequence of operations per product, with operation SAH, cycle time, manpower required, free-text workstation_code, group name, QR path, and worker input mapping — defines the production routing for a product.

nodes.name: production_day_locks
nodes.summary: Locks an entire production day, recording who locked it, when, and notes.

nodes.name: line_daily_plan_delete_markers
nodes.summary: Soft-delete marker for line daily plans, recording who deleted a line/date plan and when.

nodes.name: production_lines
nodes.summary: Master record for physical production lines including code, hall location, current product, target units, computed efficiency, QR code path, and line leader.

nodes.name: products
nodes.summary: Product master data with code, description, category, buyer name, target quantity, plan month, and the line it is assigned to.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: Application user accounts with username, full name, role (admin/supervisor/ie/management), and active flag.

nodes.name: worker_adjustments
nodes.summary: Records the response to a worker departure mid-shift — reassignment type, vacant workstation, source employee/workstation, and reassignment time.

nodes.name: worker_departures
nodes.summary: Tracks when and why an employee left their workstation mid-day (sick/personal/operational/other) for a given line/date.

nodes.name: workspaces
nodes.summary: Workspace/work-area master data per line including type, QR code path, group name, and worker input mapping.

nodes.name: v_audit_summary
nodes.summary: Database view summarizing audit_logs activity for reporting purposes.

nodes.name: v_daily_defect_summary
nodes.summary: Database view aggregating daily defect counts from defect_log for quality reporting.

nodes.name: v_daily_downtime_summary
nodes.summary: Database view aggregating daily downtime duration from downtime_log for reporting.

nodes.name: v_daily_material_summary
nodes.summary: Database view aggregating daily material transaction totals for reporting.

nodes.name: v_recent_critical_changes
nodes.summary: Database view surfacing recent critical audit log changes for monitoring.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Express route handlers exposing the MES REST API (production lines, plans, assignments, OT, reports) and the system-update endpoint.

layers.name: Middleware Layer
layers.description: Cross-cutting Express middleware for audit logging, transaction wrapping, request validation, role-based access control, and session handling that wraps every API route.

layers.name: Utility & Operations Scripts
layers.description: Shared helper modules (Excel streaming, QR generation, system-update planning) and standalone operational scripts for running migrations and applying or rolling back system updates.

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Database and app configuration modules plus project-level config files (package.json, PM2 ecosystem config, env template, ignore files) that bootstrap the runtime.

layers.name: Core Server & Realtime
layers.description: The Express application entry point and the SSE/PostgreSQL LISTEN-NOTIFY realtime broadcast module that ties API changes to live frontend updates, plus the consolidated migration tracking record.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: PostgreSQL schema definitions and the full sequence of incremental SQL migrations defining every table in the workstation-based production tracking model (plans, assignments, OT, progress, audit).

layers.name: Frontend Static Assets
layers.description: Vanilla JS/HTML/CSS single-page views for admin, supervisor, IE, and management roles, the offline-capable service worker and IndexedDB sync layer, and bundled reference documents/templates served from src/public.
